data.title: Is Poolcorp (POOL) Pricing Look Attractive After Recent Share Price Volatility?
data.description:
• This article examines whether Pool, at around US$225 per share, appears to be a bargain or a value trap by exploring what the current price may be indicating about the stock.
  • The share price has been volatile recently, with a 13.4% gain over the last 30 days, a 3.4% decline over the last week, and longer-term returns of a 1.9% decline year to date and a 21.9% decline over the past year.
  • Recent coverage has discussed Pool within broader conversations about consumer spending on home improvement and outdoor living, which can shape how investors view pool-related demand. There has also been renewed attention on how specialty distributors are priced relative to more general retail names, providing context for Pool's recent share price movements.
  • At the moment, Pool has a valuation score of 2 out of 6. The rest of this article examines how different valuation methods compare with that score and concludes with an approach to assessing value that goes beyond the usual ratios.

Pool scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Pool Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes the cash that a company is expected to generate in the future and discounts those amounts back into today’s dollars, giving an estimate of what the entire business could be worth now.

For Pool, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is about $308.1 million. Analyst inputs and subsequent extrapolations suggest projected free cash flow of $353.6 million in 2026 and $457.0 million in 2028, with further estimates extending out to 2035, all in $ and mostly in the $300 million to $500 million range when discounted back to today.

Bringing all those projected cash flows back to present value yields an estimated intrinsic value of about $272.16 per share. Compared with the current share price of around $225, the DCF output indicates the shares trade at roughly a 17.2% discount, which suggests that, on this cash flow based view, Pool appears to be undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Pool is undervalued by 17.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Pool.

Approach 2: Pool Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way for you to see how much the market is paying for each dollar of earnings. It links directly to what matters most over time, cash the business can generate from those earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how quickly earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher growth and lower perceived risk usually support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tend to justify a lower one.

Pool currently trades on a P/E of 20.46x. That is above the Retail Distributors industry average of 16.27x but below the peer group average of 37.34x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Pool is 14.76x, which is a proprietary estimate of the P/E you might expect given factors such as earnings growth, industry, profit margins, market cap and company specific risks.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for Pool’s own profile rather than assuming that every distributor or retailer deserves the same multiple. Since Pool’s actual P/E of 20.46x is higher than the Fair Ratio of 14.76x, this approach suggests the shares are pricing in a richer valuation than those fundamentals would imply.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Start investing in legacies, not executives. Discover our 18 top founder-led companies.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Pool Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Meet Narratives, a simple tool on Simply Wall St’s Community page that lets you write the story you believe about Pool, link that story to your own revenue, earnings and margin forecasts, turn those into a Fair Value, then compare that Fair Value with today’s price. The platform keeps your view updated as new news or earnings arrive. For example, one investor might lean toward a higher fair value closer to US$334 if they think recurring maintenance demand, digital tools and margin potential are compelling. Another might anchor around the lower end near US$229 if they focus more on housing, cost and competition risks. Both can see clearly how their story translates into numbers and a personal decision on whether the current price looks high, low or about right.

Do you think there's more to the story for Pool? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Stellantis Brand Shakeup Puts Jeep Ram Peugeot And Fiat At Center
data.description:
• Stellantis plans to outline a major overhaul in May that prioritizes investment in Jeep, Ram, Peugeot, and Fiat.
  • The company intends to concentrate resources on these brands and may reposition others as regional or niche players.
  • This shift reflects a response to industry pressures and rising competition, including from Chinese automakers.

Stellantis, listed as BIT:STLAM, is approaching this brand refocus with a share price of €6.836. The stock has seen a 16.2% gain over the past 30 days, set against a year-to-date decline of 29.6% and a 3-year decline of 47.0%. That mix of shorter-term strength and longer-term weakness frames investor interest in how this new plan might influence sentiment.

With Stellantis preparing to lean more heavily on Jeep, Ram, Peugeot, and Fiat, investors will be watching for detail on capital allocation, regional priorities, and potential cost implications. The May update is likely to shape how the market views BIT:STLAM’s longer-term positioning, execution risks, and the role of its non-core brands in a more focused portfolio.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Stellantis by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Stellantis.

We've flagged 1 risk for Stellantis. See which could impact your investment.

The decision to concentrate investment on Jeep, Ram, Peugeot, and Fiat marks a clear shift toward higher-volume, globally recognized brands at a time when competition from groups like Volkswagen, Toyota, and Chinese manufacturers is intense. For you as an investor, the key question is how effectively Stellantis can simplify its brand portfolio while still extracting value from smaller marques that may become regional or niche. Recent moves such as appointing a new Head of US Sales and deepening the collaboration with Microsoft on AI and software show management trying to sharpen execution and digital capabilities alongside this refocus. The planned Peugeot production push in China with Dongfeng also ties directly into the new brand priorities. Together, these steps point to a company trying to concentrate capital, technology, and management attention where it believes the strongest customer demand and brand equity sit, while managing the risk that less-favored brands lose relevance or scale.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Stellantis Narrative

  • Prioritizing Jeep, Ram, Peugeot, and Fiat supports the narrative that management is concentrating on core brands that underpin electrification plans, new BEV platforms, and software-led features.
  • Repositioning lower-volume brands as regional or niche players could challenge the growth assumptions in markets where those marques previously contributed to scale and pricing power.
  • The brand refocus, and how non-core labels are treated, may not be fully captured in existing narratives that concentrate on electrification, China partnerships, and margin improvement rather than brand rationalization detail.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Stellantis to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Concentrating investment on four brands increases dependence on their performance, so any missteps in Jeep or Ram in North America, or Peugeot and Fiat in Europe and China, could have a larger impact on group results.
  • ⚠️ Scaling back support for smaller brands may create dealer and customer friction, which can complicate execution just as Stellantis is facing competitive pressure from both established automakers and Chinese entrants.
  • 🎁 Focusing capital and management attention on the strongest marques can help streamline product lines, marketing, and R&D, which may support more disciplined spending and clearer product positioning versus peers.
  • 🎁 The brand strategy is supported by other moves such as the Microsoft AI collaboration and new leadership in US sales, which together may help Stellantis run a more data-driven, tightly managed commercial model.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on how Stellantis spells out targets and milestones at the May update, particularly around investment levels for the four focus brands, expected cost savings, and the role of smaller labels. It is also worth watching dealer responses in North America and Europe, where Jeep, Ram, Peugeot, and Fiat are central to volumes, and how the Peugeot China push with Dongfeng progresses. Shipment trends by region and segment, alongside any further leadership changes, will help show whether this brand refocus is translating into cleaner execution or adding complexity during a period of industry change.

data.title: Does InterContinental Hotels Group (LON:IHG) Deserve A Spot On Your Watchlist?
data.description:
The excitement of investing in a company that can reverse its fortunes is a big draw for some speculators, so even companies that have no revenue, no profit, and a record of falling short, can manage to find investors. But as Peter Lynch said in One Up On Wall Street, 'Long shots almost never pay off.' A loss-making company is yet to prove itself with profit, and eventually the inflow of external capital may dry up.

Despite being in the age of tech-stock blue-sky investing, many investors still adopt a more traditional strategy; buying shares in profitable companies like InterContinental Hotels Group (LON:IHG). Now this is not to say that the company presents the best investment opportunity around, but profitability is a key component to success in business.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

InterContinental Hotels Group's Earnings Per Share Are Growing

Generally, companies experiencing growth in earnings per share (EPS) should see similar trends in share price. That means EPS growth is considered a real positive by most successful long-term investors. Shareholders will be happy to know that InterContinental Hotels Group's EPS has grown 35% each year, compound, over three years. If growth like this continues on into the future, then shareholders will have plenty to smile about.

Top-line growth is a great indicator that growth is sustainable, and combined with a high earnings before interest and taxation (EBIT) margin, it's a great way for a company to maintain a competitive advantage in the market. The good news is that InterContinental Hotels Group is growing revenues, and EBIT margins improved by 2.1 percentage points to 23%, over the last year. Ticking those two boxes is a good sign of growth, in our book.

You can take a look at the company's revenue and earnings growth trend, in the chart below. Click on the chart to see the exact numbers.

View our latest analysis for InterContinental Hotels Group

Of course the knack is to find stocks that have their best days in the future, not in the past. You could base your opinion on past performance, of course, but you may also want to check this interactive graph of professional analyst EPS forecasts for InterContinental Hotels Group.

Are InterContinental Hotels Group Insiders Aligned With All Shareholders?

Since InterContinental Hotels Group has a market capitalisation of US$21b, we wouldn't expect insiders to hold a large percentage of shares. But thanks to their investment in the company, it's pleasing to see that there are still incentives to align their actions with the shareholders. Holding US$54m worth of stock in the company is no laughing matter and insiders will be committed in delivering the best outcomes for shareholders. This would indicate that the goals of shareholders and management are one and the same.

Does InterContinental Hotels Group Deserve A Spot On Your Watchlist?

For growth investors, InterContinental Hotels Group's raw rate of earnings growth is a beacon in the night. Further, the high level of insider ownership is impressive and suggests that the management appreciates the EPS growth and has faith in InterContinental Hotels Group's continuing strength. The growth and insider confidence is looked upon well and so it's worthwhile to investigate further with a view to discern the stock's true value. We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 3 warning signs for InterContinental Hotels Group (2 shouldn't be ignored!) that you need to be mindful of.

There's always the possibility of doing well buying stocks that are not growing earnings and do not have insiders buying shares. But for those who consider these important metrics, we encourage you to check out companies that do have those features. data.description:

  • The stock has returned 1.1% over the last 7 days and 12.3% over the last month, while the year-to-date return sits at a 33.8% decline and the 1-year return is 51.3%.

MongoDB scores just 1/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: MongoDB Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows, then discounts them back to today using a required rate of return. The goal is to arrive at an estimate of what the business might be worth per share based purely on those projected cash flows.

For MongoDB, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, working entirely in US$. The latest twelve month free cash flow sits at about $488.9 million. Analysts provide explicit free cash flow estimates out to 2031, where projections show free cash flow of $1.32b. Simply Wall St then extrapolates beyond the initial analyst window to complete a 10 year path of cash flows.

Discounting those projected cash flows back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $275.78 per share. Against a current share price of around $264, the DCF output points to roughly a 4.1% discount, which is treated here as essentially in line rather than a clear bargain or a clear premium.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

MongoDB is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for MongoDB.

Approach 2: MongoDB Price vs Sales

For a business where earnings are not yet a clean guide, revenue can be a more practical yardstick. This makes the P/S ratio a useful way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of MongoDB’s sales.

In general, higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher “normal” valuation multiple. Slower growth or higher uncertainty tend to pull that multiple down. MongoDB currently trades on a P/S of 8.62x. That sits above the wider IT industry average of 1.80x and also above the peer group average of 6.68x, so on simple comparisons the stock looks expensive relative to both its sector and similar companies.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework tries to refine this picture. It estimates what a reasonable P/S could be for MongoDB, at 7.75x, after accounting for factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, risk profile, market cap and industry. Because this Fair Ratio is tailored to the company’s own characteristics, it can be more informative than broad peer or industry averages alone. Comparing 8.62x to the 7.75x Fair Ratio suggests the shares are pricing in a higher valuation than this model implies.

Result: OVERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, and Narratives on Simply Wall St are exactly that: a clear story you choose about MongoDB that ties your view on its role in AI data, regulation and competition to specific forecasts for revenue, earnings and margins. The platform then turns these into a Fair Value that you can easily compare to the current share price to see whether your story points you toward being a buyer, a holder or a seller. Because Narratives on the Community page update automatically when new earnings, guidance or news arrive, you can see in real time how a cautious MongoDB Narrative with a Fair Value around US$250 and a more optimistic MongoDB Narrative with a Fair Value around US$462 can both coexist. This gives you a simple way to decide which story, and which Fair Value range, fits your own view of the company.

For MongoDB, however, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading MongoDB Narratives:

First is a higher fair value scenario that leans into the AI data opportunity and application modernization story.

🐂 MongoDB Bull Case

Fair Value: US$462.19

Implied discount to this Fair Value: about 42.8% compared to the last close of US$264.38

Revenue growth assumption: 20.6% a year

  • Analysts in this camp see MongoDB benefiting from strong customer and workload growth, with Atlas, AI features and modernization tools helping it win more workloads and displace legacy databases.
  • They expect expansion across industries and regions to broaden the revenue base and improve earnings quality over time, as more of the business comes from diversified, recurring contracts.
  • To line up with this Fair Value, you would need to be comfortable with revenue of about US$4.3b by 2029, earnings of about US$339.8m and a P/E near 135x on those earnings, using a discount rate of roughly 9%.

The second narrative leans more on the risks around regulation, competition and hyperscaler power, and lands on a much lower Fair Value.

🐻 MongoDB Bear Case

Fair Value: US$250.00

Implied premium to this Fair Value: about 5.8% compared to the last close of US$264.38

  • This group is more focused on rising data regulation, open source and MongoDB compatible rivals, and the influence of large cloud providers, all of which could pressure margins and cap long term growth.
  • They model solid revenue growth but assume it takes longer for profitability to settle at industry like levels, so the business would need to support earnings of about US$304.3m by 2029 and a P/E a little above 81x on those earnings.
  • If you side with this view, MongoDB at around US$264 already sits above the Fair Value anchor, so the key questions are whether growth, AI monetization and Atlas usage can justify paying that sort of multiple.

data.title: Scorpio Tankers Reshapes Fleet And Earnings Mix With Vessel Sales And Orders
data.description:
• Scorpio Tankers (NYSE:STNG) has agreed to sell six vessels for roughly $300 million as part of a wider fleet reshuffle.
  • The company is pairing these disposals with newbuilding orders for both product and crude tankers, indicating a renewed fleet profile.
  • The combined actions represent a significant repositioning of Scorpio Tankers’ asset base and earnings mix.

For investors tracking NYSE:STNG, this fleet repositioning comes with the stock at $79.74 and returns of 60.5% year to date and 125.0% over the past year. Over the last 5 years, the share price performance of 360.1% places Scorpio Tankers among the more aggressively rerated shipping names in the market.

Active fleet renewal through vessel sales and newbuild commitments can reshape Scorpio Tankers’ cost base, age profile and exposure across product and crude trades. Investors who follow shipping cycles often watch these capital allocation decisions closely, as they can affect earnings sensitivity and how the company competes against peers.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Scorpio Tankers by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Scorpio Tankers.

📰 Beyond the headline: 4 risks and 2 things going right for Scorpio Tankers that every investor should see.

This fleet reshuffle is a sizeable move for Scorpio Tankers because it swaps nine 2014 built product tankers for a pipeline of newer MR and LR2 ships plus two very large crude carriers (VLCCs). On the disposal side, roughly US$300 million of agreed vessel sales and the repayment of around US$32 million of related revolving credit gives the company extra financial flexibility and slightly lowers balance sheet risk. On the growth side, committing to ten newbuildings, including crude exposure through VLCCs, gradually tilts the asset base and earnings mix away from a pure product tanker story.

For you as an investor, the trade off is clear. In the near term, the company will have fewer on the water vessels, which can cap spot exposure and rate upside during the transition. Over 2026 to 2029, the delivery schedule introduces new capacity that could support earnings if product and crude markets stay supportive. It also raises capital spending and execution risk. Against a context of flat long term revenue, a shrinking fleet and earnings per share pressure, this repositioning reads as an attempt to refresh the portfolio rather than simply grow for growth’s sake.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Scorpio Tankers Narrative

  • The emphasis on younger, more efficient tonnage aligns with the narrative that a modern fleet can capture trade flow shifts and support margins as refining patterns change.
  • Adding VLCCs and increasing order book exposure introduces more sensitivity to future capacity cycles, which the narrative already flags as a threat to long term profitability.
  • The specific timing and scale of these newbuilding commitments, including deliveries out to 2029, are not fully reflected in the existing narrative’s discussion of future capex needs.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Scorpio Tankers to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts highlight that earnings are forecast to decline by an average of 3.3% per year over the next 3 years, so higher capital commitments add pressure on future returns.
  • ⚠️ Rising fleet age related costs and the broader risk of tanker overcapacity could weigh on utilization and margins once this larger order book delivers into the market.
  • 🎁 Trading at what is assessed as 31.4% below an estimate of fair value gives some room for the fleet reshuffle to add value if execution and market conditions line up.
  • 🎁 The company is described as having high quality earnings and trading at good value compared with peers, which may support investor confidence while the fleet mix is reset.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, keep an eye on three things. First, the actual sale prices, closing timeline and any extra debt reduction tied to the nine disposals. Second, the progress and cost discipline on MR, LR2 and VLCC newbuild deliveries through 2026 to 2029, especially if product tanker peers such as Torm and Hafnia or crude focused names like Frontline pursue similar ordering. Third, how this leaner but younger fleet affects Scorpio Tankers’ revenue, margins and cash flow in future results, given the recent pattern of flat long term revenue and softer earnings per share. Any change in analyst risk flags, such as the current set of 2 rewards and 4 risks, will also be useful context as the repositioning plays out.
